objective: Add a no-secret Remote MCP operator inbound message preflight that proves fixture receive through the HITL relay session inbox and fails closed when live queue/session configuration is absent.
scope_boundary: Modify the Remote MCP operator inbound preflight script, focused tests, runbook, governance registries, progress/backlog mirrors, issue #382 planning/validation/closeout artifacts, preflight evidence, and generated graph/wiki artifacts.
Reuse the existing HITL relay queue and validator contracts; do not add a new live push transport.
Do not commit Cloudflare credentials, bridge tokens, JWTs, HMAC keys, raw PII, raw operator message bodies, or production payloads.
out_of_scope: Configuring production SMS, Slack, terminal push, or Remote MCP live inbound transport.
Installing or changing local launchd jobs.
Claiming live inbound receive readiness when queue root/session configuration is absent.
Changing HITL relay packet schema semantics.
research_summary: Issue #380 proved request/response readiness for `som.ping` but explicitly did not prove inbound operator messaging. The existing HITL relay queue already validates and writes `session_instruction` packets to `session-inbox`; issue #382 adds a dedicated preflight and runbook path around that receive contract.

sprints.name: Operator inbound preflight
sprints.goal: Provide a no-secret fixture/live preflight for operator-targeted message receive readiness through the HITL relay session inbox path.
tasks: Move #380 from active mirrors to Done and mark #382 active.
Add the operator inbound preflight script with fixture and live modes.
Add focused tests for fixture receive, live missing-config fail-closed behavior, configured queue without instruction, and CLI JSON output.
Update runbook, service registry, feature registry, and data dictionary surfaces.
Generate fixture and current-machine live fail-closed evidence.
Run local gates, open PR, merge after checks pass, and close #382.
acceptance_criteria: Backlog/progress mirrors track #382 as active before implementation.
Preflight emits JSON with ready, mode, checks, warnings, missing live config, and recommended action without secret values.
Fixture e2e proves an operator-targeted message is received in a session inbox through the HITL relay queue path.
Live e2e either proves a configured inbound message receive path or fails closed before attempting live receive when required setup is missing.
Runbook clearly distinguishes request/response (`som.ping`) from inbound/operator-message receive capability.
Evidence scan proves preserved artifacts contain no raw SSNs, bearer-token material, Cloudflare Access markers, JWT fragments, credentials, or raw message bodies.
Local tests and Local CI Gate pass.
Final AC: GitHub PR checks pass before closeout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm issue #382 can reuse existing HITL relay queue/session-inbox contracts without adding a push transport or reading raw message bodies.
specialist_reviews.summary: Accepted a scoped fixture/live preflight around `hitl_relay_queue.py`, with live mode requiring only queue root and session id and no secret values.

alternate_model_review.summary: Accepted the #382 scope: request/response remains #380, inbound receive is proved through the HITL relay queue fixture and live fail-closed config checks.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #382 may update the Remote MCP inbound preflight script, focused tests, runbook, registries, backlog/progress mirrors, preflight evidence, validation, closeout, and generated graph/wiki artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the operator inbound preflight, validate locally, open PR, merge after checks pass, and close issue #382.

material_deviation_rules: If any preserved artifact includes secrets, JWT fragments, Cloudflare Access material, bearer-token material, raw PII, or raw message bodies, remove the artifact and rerun from sanitized inputs.
If production live queue/session configuration is absent, preserve live fail-closed evidence only; do not claim live inbound receive readiness.
If live queue inspection finds no validated instruction, report `ready: false` without reading or printing raw message bodies.
